findings.description: With visible=0 the len(value)<=0 guard is skipped for non-empty input and value[-0:] equals value[0:], so the result is a full mask string with the entire original value appended, leaking the full secret.
value[-0:] is value[0:] (the whole string) rather than an empty tail, making mask_middle return mask+full-value for visible=0 instead of a masked string.
For value length just above visible*2 (e.g. visible=4, len=9) only one character is masked, exposing nearly the entire value despite the 'avoid leaking most of the content' contract.
A negative max_length makes slug[:max_length] slice from the end, producing a wrong/garbage-length slug instead of an empty or full one.
levenshtein has no input-length cap; O(len(a)*len(b)) work on untrusted long strings is a potential resource-exhaustion vector.
indent_block uses splitlines(), which splits on Unicode boundaries (\v, \f, \u2028) and drops a trailing newline, diverging from \n-only line handling for a text formatter.
A negative spaces value yields ''*spaces == '' and silently no-ops instead of raising, unlike mask_middle's negative-arg guard.
